prompt:
Create a FlowCanvas Offline Studio for product managers, business analysts, and implementation specialists who need to sketch workflows, page prototypes, and lightweight low-code diagrams in air-gapped or no-internet environments. The app opens into a local pseudo-login screen (username + passphrase verified only within the browser) with a 30-minute inactivity auto-logout timer; failed sign-ins trigger a local 15-minute UI cooldown after 3 attempts per profile (a deterrent only, not a security boundary because clearing browser storage resets it). After login, users land on a Project List view (max 50 projects), where each project can contain up to 20 canvases shown in a Table/Card toggle with quick search, tags, and pinned items. The canvas editor is a zoomable infinite surface with draggable nodes, routed connection lines, snap-to-grid, alignment guides, grouping, multi-select, and a right-side Inspector Drawer for properties. It includes exactly eight built-in components (Text, Button, Input, Image, Container, Label, Flow Node, Sticky Note) and enforces a hard limit of 5,000 elements per canvas; exceeding the limit must show a blocking Modal explaining the cap and preventing further adds. Editing supports at least 200 undo/redo steps, autosaves every 10 seconds, retains the most recent 30 versions per canvas, and allows one-click rollback with a confirmation dialog. Bulk import accepts user-selected local JSON/CSV files (up to 1,000 nodes per import) with row-level validation (required: id, type, x, y; optional: text, width, height); duplicate IDs are auto-renamed using an incremented suffix (e.g., "step_3"), and a Notification Center message lists all changes. Export supports PNG, SVG, and JSON; images used by Image components must be picked locally and are limited to 50 MB per file. UI personas include Admin (manages dictionaries/enums, template channels/topics/tags, pins/featured slots, and announcements), Editor (builds canvases and imports/exports), and Reviewer (adds reviews and change tickets); roles only filter menus and screens for convenience, not enforcement.

Built as an Angular + TypeScript single-page application, all logic runs in-browser with no network calls. LocalStorage stores lightweight preferences (theme, grid size, hotkey profile, last-open project, feature toggles), while IndexedDB persists projects, canvases, versions, audit logs, reviews, and file blobs (embedded images, exports, and ticket attachments). User-initiated backup/restore is provided via JSON bundle export and file pick/import using the File API plus Blob downloads (FileSaver-style), with size checks and clear error toasts. A Service Worker caches app assets for installable offline use, Web Workers handle heavy operations (large imports, SVG/PNG rendering, version compaction) to keep interaction responsive, and BroadcastChannel coordinates multi-tab editing by warning on concurrent edits and reconciling "latest save wins" with a visible conflict banner. An in-app Diagnostics screen acts as local observability: storage quota/usage, IndexedDB health check, render FPS sampling, action tracing for slow operations, and threshold alerts (e.g., warn at 80% of element cap or 75% of storage budget), with all events recorded into an immutable local audit timeline for later review.
